summary: Completed comprehensive testing of QuickBooks-level accounting features for Hishab Nikash Pro. Backend APIs 100% functional with all 45 tests passing. Chart of Accounts (19 accounts), Journal Entries (double-entry validation), Estimates (convert-to-invoice), Bills (AP with payments), Receive Stock, General Ledger, Trial Balance, and Receive Payment Bulk all working perfectly. Frontend authentication issue prevents UI testing.

integration_issues.issue: Test session token not working in browser automation - prevents UI testing of new features

passed_tests: Backend: Authentication with test session token (API level)
Backend: Chart of Accounts - 19 default accounts created and accessible
Backend: Chart of Accounts - Create new account functionality
Backend: Journal Entries - Get all journal entries
Backend: Journal Entries - Create balanced journal entry (debits = credits)
Backend: Journal Entries - Correctly reject unbalanced entries (validation working)
Backend: Estimates - Get all estimates
Backend: Estimates - Create new estimate
Backend: Estimates - Convert estimate to invoice functionality
Backend: Bills - Get all bills
Backend: Bills - Create new bill
Backend: Bills - Pay bill (partial payment functionality)
Backend: Receive Stock - Get all stock receipts
Backend: Receive Stock - Create stock receipt with inventory updates
Backend: General Ledger - Get ledger with all accounts and transactions
Backend: General Ledger - Account filtering functionality
Backend: General Ledger - Date range filtering
Backend: Trial Balance - Get balanced trial balance (debits = credits)
Backend: Trial Balance - Date filtering functionality
Backend: Receive Payment Bulk - Process bulk payment to multiple invoices
Backend: All existing APIs (Products CRUD, AI Assistant, Settings, Team Management)
Backend: Low stock alerts and scheduled tasks
Backend: Balance Sheet and Cash Flow reports
Backend: Customer statements
action_item_for_main_agent: Frontend authentication flow needs investigation for automated testing. Test session token works for API calls but not for browser-based authentication. All backend functionality is working perfectly.
